titles.title: PyTurbo_SF: An Adaptive Bootstrap Framework for Efficient Structure Function Analysis in Turbulent Flows

institution.institution_name: Department of Earth, Environmental, and Planetary Sciences (DEEPS), Brown University, Providence, RI, USA
Department of Earth, Environmental, and Planetary Sciences (DEEPS), Brown University, Providence, RI, USA

citation.volume_title: Turbulence: The legacy of A.N. Kolmogorov
citation.author: Frisch
citation.unstructured_citation: Frisch, U. (1995). Turbulence: The legacy of A.N. Kolmogorov. Cambridge University Press. https://doi.org/10.1017/CBO9781139170666

citation.author: Pope
citation.unstructured_citation: Pope, S. B. (2000). Turbulent flows. Cambridge University Press. https://doi.org/10.1017/CBO9780511840531

citation.article_title: Advective structure functions in anisotropic two-dimensional turbulence
citation.author: Pearson
citation.unstructured_citation: Pearson, B., Pearson, J., & Fox-Kemper, B. (2021). Advective structure functions in anisotropic two-dimensional turbulence. Journal of Fluid Mechanics, 916, A49. https://doi.org/10.1017/jfm.2021.247

citation.article_title: Estimating spectral fluxes in quasi-two-dimensional flows with advective structure functions and Bessel functions
citation.author: Pearson
citation.unstructured_citation: Pearson, B., Wagner, C., Fox-Kemper, B., & Samelson, R. (2024). Estimating spectral fluxes in quasi-two-dimensional flows with advective structure functions and Bessel functions. Journal of Physical Oceanography. https://doi.org/10.1175/jpo-d-24-0211.1

citation.article_title: fastSF: A parallel code for computing the structure functions of turbulence
citation.author: Sadhukhan
citation.unstructured_citation: Sadhukhan, S., Bhattacharya, S., & Verma, M. K. (2021). fastSF: A parallel code for computing the structure functions of turbulence. Journal of Open Source Software, 6(57), 2185. https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.02185

citation.article_title: An open source package to perform basic and advanced statistical analysis of turbulence data and other complex systems
citation.author: Fuchs
citation.unstructured_citation: Fuchs, A., Kharche, S., Patil, A., Friedrich, J., Wächter, M., & Peinke, J. (2022). An open source package to perform basic and advanced statistical analysis of turbulence data and other complex systems. Physics of Fluids, 34(10), 101801. https://doi.org/10.1063/5.0107974

citation.article_title: FlowSieve: A coarse-graining utility for geophysical flows on the sphere
citation.author: Storer
citation.unstructured_citation: Storer, B. A., & Aluie, H. (2023). FlowSieve: A coarse-graining utility for geophysical flows on the sphere. Journal of Open Source Software, 8(84), 4277. https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.04277

citation.article_title: Global observations of fine-scale ocean surface topography with the Surface Water and Ocean Topography (SWOT) mission
citation.author: Morrow
citation.unstructured_citation: Morrow, R., Fu, L.-L., Ardhuin, F., Benkiran, M., Chapron, B., Cosme, E., d’Ovidio, F., Farrar, J. T., Gille, S. T., Lapeyre, G., & others. (2019). Global observations of fine-scale ocean surface topography with the Surface Water and Ocean Topography (SWOT) mission. Frontiers in Marine Science, 6, 232. https://doi.org/10.3389/fmars.2019.00232

citation.article_title: The regional oceanic modeling system (ROMS): A split-explicit, free-surface, topography-following-coordinate oceanic model
citation.author: Shchepetkin
citation.unstructured_citation: Shchepetkin, A. F., & McWilliams, J. C. (2005). The regional oceanic modeling system (ROMS): A split-explicit, free-surface, topography-following-coordinate oceanic model. Ocean Modelling, 9(4), 347–404. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ocemod.2004.08.002

citation.article_title: Evaluation of large-eddy simulations via observations of nocturnal marine stratocumulus
citation.author: Stevens
citation.unstructured_citation: Stevens, B., Moeng, C.-H., Ackerman, A. S., Bretherton, C. S., Chlond, A., Roode, S. de, Edwards, J., Golaz, J.-C., Jiang, H., Khairoutdinov, M., & others. (2005). Evaluation of large-eddy simulations via observations of nocturnal marine stratocumulus. Monthly Weather Review, 133(6), 1443–1462. https://doi.org/10.1175/MWR2930.1

citation.article_title: FluidSF: A Python package for calculating turbulent flow statistics
citation.author: Wagner
citation.unstructured_citation: Wagner, C. M., Lee, A., & Pearson, B. (2025). FluidSF: A Python package for calculating turbulent flow statistics. Journal of Open Source Software, 10(114), 7873. https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.07873
